compressed_prompt: Compose song lyrics about a socio-economic problem. The song should be in English and in all lowercase letters.
response:
# **"empty pockets, full streets"**

*(verse 1)*
the alarm goes off at half past four
worn-out boots walking out the door
two jobs bleeding into one long day
still can't keep the landlord's voice away

the kids need shoes, the lights need paying
the bank account keeps slowly fading
working harder than my father did
but the ladder's missing every rung and grid

*(chorus)*
empty pockets, full streets tonight
everybody grinding just to survive
the numbers don't add up, never did
when the rent keeps rising but the wages sit still
empty pockets, full streets tonight
we're not lazy, we're just losing the fight

*(verse 2)*
the grocery store raised prices again
choosing between the medicine and bread
the degree is hanging on the wall
but the student debt is ten feet tall

the landlord bought his seventh property
while we're splitting one room, three of us, see
the system wasn't broken, it was built this way
designed to keep the working hands from getting paid

*(chorus)*

*(bridge)*
we are not invisible
we are not disposable
we are the hands that built this town
we deserve more than being run into the ground

*(outro)*
empty pockets
but our voices are full
we're still here
we're still here
